kills: CFC's depth-accounting plausibility core is self-contradictory. The proposal claims rainbow-forcing a two-circle set needs 'import depth >= 2' while 'the only proven Euclidean import mechanism has depth 1' and 'de Grey imported depth 1 onto brittle 4-colorings'. False by its own admission that the 4-color analog of CFC is false: a 5-vertex-critical subgraph of de Grey's graph minus a vertex rainbow-forces all 4 colors onto a single-circle link section whose internal chi is <= 2 (sections are unions of paths and C6s, bipartite). That is deficit 4-2 = 2, already realized Euclidean technology at k=4. The claimed asymmetry (need 2, only 1 exists) is arithmetic error, and with it strand (iv) of why_plausible collapses.

CFC strand (iii) ('at 5 colors every probe ever run finds zero rigidity; rainbow-forcing and realizability have never coexisted') is contradicted by the repo's own record. L27 found 97 sets U_v of size 22-27 inside P_510, a REALIZED chi-5 UDG, that carried all 5 colors in all 88 sampled proper 5-colorings (experiments/LEARNINGS.md lines 826-875). L57's freedom is pair-level only; set-level rainbow forcing empirically exists at k=5 inside the realized lineage. What has never been observed is such a set lying on one or two unit circles (the 0/97 cocircularity failures). CFC is therefore a geometric-placement conjecture, not 'the law behind the freedom evidence'. Also a citation error: 0/92 is L29's mixed-half 510x553 graph, not L28.

Definability Split prediction (iii) is a category error with a historical refutation. 'A finitely-supported optimal dual would transfer the bound to a finite UDG and contradict (i)' is false: the measurable SDP's constraints (density, autocorrelation, O(2)-averaged J_0 Bochner, IEC) are valid only for measurable colorings, so finitely-supported certificates bound chi_m, not chi. Live counterexample in-repo: the chi_m >= 5 certificate runs on the 23-point X_23 (L36 1/4-crossing) while no 23-vertex UDG comes near chi 5. Falconer proved chi_m >= 5 from finite configurations in 1981, 37 years before any finite chi >= 5 UDG existed. The split's only near-term falsifiable probe tests nothing.

The experiment's 'DECISIVE BOTH WAYS' claim is false. The consensus position (L45/L56/L57: the missing object is provably absent from the lineage and must come from a NEW chi-5 UDG) also predicts all-SAT on all 12 lineage graphs for the rich-center scan. All-SAT therefore does not discriminate CFC from the consensus and is not 'a material posterior shift from chi=6 toward chi=5'. Only a hit is informative, and a hit refutes the contrarian. CFC has NO lineage-domain prediction that differs from the consensus; its only distinguishing content is the final answer itself.

Pair-pass hit overclaim: a hit in a rigid lineage realization yields a clamp at one fixed distance d. K6 in the two-distance graph G_{1,d} is impossible (planar two-distance sets have at most 5 points, Larman-Rogers), so a single rigid clamp does not give chi >= 6 'outright'; the frontier's own qualifier is FLEXIBLE clamp (L53 caveat (d): generically minimally rigid H-w has zero flex). Only the single-circle apex hit is outright (R + apex is a 6-chromatic UDG). 'Any hit = chi-6 UDG outright' is true only for pass (2).

KM5 is doubly disconnected from CFC: (a) absence of frozen colorings does not imply Kempe mixing (multiple Kempe classes can exist with none frozen; frozenness is sufficient, not necessary, for non-mixing), and (b) Kempe mixing does not preclude rainbow-forcing (moves can permute which colors sit on S while all 5 stay present; the L27 U_v sets are exactly this shape). So even if both forms of KM5 hold, CFC does not follow. The proposal half-admits this, then still sells KM5 as 'the proposed mechanism behind CFC'. Also note the m >= 4n-10 bound must be supplemented by min-degree >= 4 (a degree <= 3 vertex is isolated in some class-pair union), which the proposal misses.

Pair-pass coverage gap: Theorem R clamps need only |A|+|B| >= 5 with |A| or |B| as small as 1 or 2 (e.g. a (1,4) split is a rainbow-forced 4-set on one circle plus a singleton), but the scan filters to pairs of >= 3-rich centers. Splits (1,k) and (2,k) are silently excluded, so all-SAT would not even close the lineage in 'the FULL Theorem R sense' as claimed.

Exact-arithmetic sloppiness and cost optimism: intersection points of two unit circles with centers in Q(sqrt3,sqrt11) live in quadratic extensions (sqrt(4-d^2) terms), not in the lineage field as stated; exact dedup needs minimal-polynomial canonicalization or high-precision hashing with exact confirmation on collisions. Incidence counting is O(n^3)-ish (~6.7e8 mpmath evaluations for the 874-vertex graph), so 'minutes' is wrong without a numpy float prefilter; the weekend estimate is reachable only with that engineering.

Novelty overclaims: CFC corollary (a) (6-critical minus a vertex rainbow-forces its link circle, and conversely apex addition) is the standard criticality/link mechanism underlying de Grey's own construction and Polymath16's toolkit; the 'appears nowhere in the literature' claim is packaging. In-repo, L42's f1pt_attack1d already SAT-scanned cocircular-at-unit 5-subsets of P_510 (vertex-centered), so 'no logged experiment has done this' needs the non-vertex-center and |S|>=6 qualifiers to be true. The Moser circumradius calibration (3/sqrt(11)) is verified correct, but one bad triple does not by itself prove the spindle is not two-circle-supportable; that claim needs all 2-partitions checked.
prior_art_collisions: de Grey 2018 / Polymath16: link-circle criticality analysis is the standard mechanism there; CFC corollary (a) repackages it, and de Grey's k=4 link forcing (4 colors forced onto a bipartite circle section) is precisely the depth-2 import that breaks the proposal's depth heuristic.

Larman-Rogers / Erdos: planar two-distance sets have at most 5 points, so no K6 in G_{1,d}; blocks any 'rigid clamp gives chi >= 6 outright' reading; completion needs flex or two-forbidden-distance chromatic results (Exoo-Ismailescu lineage).
Falconer 1981 and Ambrus-Matolcsi X_23 (repo L36/L43): finite-support measurable certificates do not transfer to finite UDG chromatic bounds; this kills the split's prediction (iii) outright.

Kempe literature: Meyniel 1978 (all 5-colorings of planar graphs Kempe-connected) does NOT apply since UDGs are non-planar abstract graphs; Las Vergnas-Meyniel degeneracy theorems do not apply since UDGs are not boundedly degenerate (projected hypercube Minkowski sums give k-regular UDGs for any k); Mohar's conjecture / Bonamy-Bousquet-Feghali-Johnson / Feghali-Johnson-Paulusma (3-prism frozen 3-colorings, and the 3-prism IS a UDG, though at k=3 not k=5). KM5 is genuinely unsettled by prior art, but also unleveraged by it.

Repo-internal: L42 f1pt_attack1c/1d already attacked rainbow-forcing of cocircular-at-unit 5-sets in P_510 and the triangular lattice (vertex centers, |S|=5 regime); L27/L28/L29 cocircularity sieves (0/97, 0/92) are the actual source of the proposal's headline statistic.

Shelah-Soifer 2003, Voronov map-type 7, and the repo's own L54 col5/borel worlds: the Definability Split is their explicit fusion (the proposal concedes); L54 already flagged the chi_B=7 upper half as having no detector-passing blade.

Chilakamarri / Petty: TC's Delta <= 4 cap and the <= 2-point circle intersection hold in every strictly convex planar norm (Petty: equilateral sets <= 3 unless the ball is a parallelogram), so only the 60-degree/C6 bipartite structure is genuinely Euclid-specific; TC's provable part is strict-convexity-generic, mildly weakening the 'Euclid-only' framing.
result.surviving_kernel: The rich-center rainbow scan, stripped of its contrarian framing, is a genuine and well-posed gap-closer: L45/L56/L57 classified only pairs of EXISTING vertices, while Theorem R's native object lives at non-vertex circle centers, and both core mechanisms verify correctly (one SAT query per center by S_5 color symmetry decides rainbow-forcing; a single-circle UNSAT plus apex yields a |V|+1-vertex 6-chromatic UDG; the pair-pass extendability query, forbid color 4 on A and color 5 on B, correctly certifies a realizable clamp since forced-different(s,t) is exactly rainbow-forcing of A union B). Also surviving: the |S|>=6 floor is rigorously justified (a rainbow-forced 5-set of vertices would contain a non-adjacent forced-different pair, contradicting L57, or be a unit-K5, contradicting omega=3); CFC's single-circle case as a sharp reformulation (chi(R^2) <= 5 iff no chi <= 5 UDG rainbow-forces a unit-circle section) is correct and crisp even though it is logically equivalent to the position it argues for; TC's provable core (chi <= 4 on two-circle sets via the degree-4 cap and Brooks) is correct and the chi=3-vs-4 question is a clean Groebner-feasible standalone, valuable to the CONSENSUS program too since a 4-chromatic two-circle configuration would be the first locally-chi-4 interface; and the frozen-coloring census on cached SAT witnesses is a cheap new observable (with the corrected necessary conditions m >= 4n-10 AND min degree >= 4).
result.cheapest_decisive_test: The S199 single-circle pass alone: enumerate all pairwise unit-circle intersection points (numpy float64 prefilter, mpmath 50-digit confirm, exact spot-check on collisions), keep non-vertex centers with >= 6 incident vertices, then one cadical query per center forbidding color 5 on the section. About 200 lines, under 2 hours. A single UNSAT is a ~200-vertex 6-chromatic UDG (kills Position A, ends the program); all-SAT closes Theorem R's native single-circle form on S199 and, per the consensus-predicts-it-too argument, must be logged as zero posterior shift. The free side-product (frozen-coloring check on the same cached witnesses) decides KM5's non-vacuity in minutes.
result.steelman: Drop the chi=5 advocacy, the depth heuristic, and the split's prediction (iii), and reframe as: 'Theorem R has never been tested in its native geometric form: the lineage sweeps (L45/L56/L57) only probed pairs of existing vertices, but the clamp's endpoints s,t are NEW vertices at circle centers. Here is the exact scan: (1) single-circle pass over all non-vertex rich centers with the one-query color-symmetry trick, where any UNSAT is immediately a |V|+1-vertex 6-chromatic UDG; (2) two-circle pair pass with FULL split coverage including (1,k) and (2,k) splits (one side allowed incidence 1 or 2 when |S| >= 6), sample-filtered against ~300 cached colorings, dual-solver UNSAT discipline, where a hit is the program's W3 object at a rigid distance d, to be followed by the flex/two-distance completion question rather than claimed as chi >= 6 outright. Log the honest evidential statement up front: both CFC and the consensus predict all-SAT on the lineage, so the scan's value is the hit-chance plus completeness, not posterior movement. Separately run two standalone structure probes: TC (is chi=4 attained on two unit circles? Groebner-decidable to n=12, informative for the wide-interface route either way) and the frozen-5-coloring census (new observable, correct necessary conditions m >= 4n-10 and min degree >= 4). Record CFC's single-circle case as the canonical reformulation of chi <= 5, replacing the depth-1/depth-2 story with the true calibration: deficit-2 import exists at k=4 (de Grey links), set-level rainbow forcing exists empirically at k=5 in P_510 (L27, sample-level, worth SAT-certifying one U_v as a follow-up), and the open question is purely whether forcing can be steered onto one or two unit circles.'
